title: Christ's Words On The Cross: My God, My God.
content:
Christ, when He hung the dreadful cross upon,
    Had, as it were, a dereliction
    In this regard, in those great terrors He
    Had no one beam from God's sweet majesty.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Christ's Words On The Cross: My God, My God."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
